remember: Students will be able to define and identify different types of natural hazards and disasters.
Students will be able to recall the causes and effects of various natural hazards and disasters.
Students will be able to list the different types of natural hazards and disasters and their distribution across the world.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of natural hazards and disasters and their impact on the environment and human societies.
Students will be able to describe the relationship between natural hazards and disasters and the geographical factors that contribute to their occurrence.
Students will be able to interpret maps and data related to natural hazards and disasters.
apply: Students will be able to apply their knowledge of natural hazards and disasters to real-life situations and case studies.
Students will be able to suggest measures to mitigate and manage the impact of natural hazards and disasters.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of human activities in increasing the vulnerability to natural hazards and disasters.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the factors that contribute to the occurrence and impact of natural hazards and disasters.
Students will be able to compare and contrast different types of natural hazards and disasters and their effects on the environment and human societies.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of different strategies for disaster management and mitigation.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1, to understand the concept of natural hazards and disasters and their impact on the environment and human societies.
As per NCERT objective 2, to identify the different types of natural hazards and disasters and their distribution across the world.
As per NCERT objective 3, to analyze the factors that contribute to the occurrence and impact of natural hazards and disasters.
board_exam_objectives: What CBSE tests from this chapter is the ability to define and explain key terms related to natural hazards and disasters.
CBSE also tests the ability to identify and describe the causes and effects of different types of natural hazards and disasters.
The ability to suggest measures to mitigate and manage the impact of natural hazards and disasters is also tested in the board exam.
